description:
You are a network engineer analysing a raw ping log to find out which destination 
addresses are persistently unreachable.  
A text file already exists at  

    /home/user/network_logs/ping_results.log

Your goal is to generate a concise, machine-readable report that lists every IP
address that returned the status **FAILED**, together with the number of times
that address failed, sorted from the most to the least frequent failure.

1. Extract only the IP addresses that have the word “FAILED” at the end of the
   line (case-sensitive match).
2. Count how many times each of those IPs occurs.
3. Sort the result by:
   a. descending numerical frequency;  
   b. if two IPs have the same frequency, ascending (alphabetical) order of the
      IP string.
4. Write the final, sorted list into  

       /home/user/network_logs/unreachable_report.txt

   using the **exact** format shown below — one single space between the IP
   address and the count, no leading or trailing spaces, no header line, UNIX
   newline at the end of every line (including the last one):

       10.0.0.1 3
       10.0.0.2 2
       172.16.0.1 2

5. After creating the file, display its contents on the terminal.

Only standard GNU coreutils (e.g. grep, awk, cut, sort, uniq) are needed; the
entire task can be completed with two or three pipeline commands.  Do **not**
modify the original ping_results.log file.  Do **not** remove or rename any
files.  When you are finished, the automated grader will:

• verify that /home/user/network_logs/unreachable_report.txt exists  
• check that its contents match the exact format and ordering specified above.
truth:
Initial file (already present before the task starts):
Path: /home/user/network_logs/ping_results.log
Contents (exactly 11 lines, UNIX newlines):

[2023-10-01 12:00:01] 192.168.1.1 SUCCESS
[2023-10-01 12:00:02] 10.0.0.1 FAILED
[2023-10-01 12:00:03] 192.168.1.1 SUCCESS
[2023-10-01 12:00:04] 10.0.0.1 FAILED
[2023-10-01 12:00:05] 172.16.0.1 FAILED
[2023-10-01 12:00:06] 10.0.0.1 FAILED
[2023-10-01 12:00:07] 172.16.0.1 FAILED
[2023-10-01 12:00:08] 192.168.1.1 SUCCESS
[2023-10-01 12:00:09] 10.0.0.2 FAILED
[2023-10-01 12:00:10] 10.0.0.2 FAILED
[2023-10-01 12:00:11] 192.168.1.2 SUCCESS


File that must be created by the agent:
Path: /home/user/network_logs/unreachable_report.txt
Required contents (exactly 3 lines, UNIX newlines):

10.0.0.1 3
10.0.0.2 2
172.16.0.1 2

The grader will assert that unreachable_report.txt exists with these precise
contents and that ping_results.log remains unchanged.
